639	\subsection*{The main \(\chi\)-identity}
640	
641	\begin{theorem}
642	
643	
644	\leanok
645	[Euler-characteristic identity for \(\mathcal O_C(D)\)]
646	  \label{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus}
647	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus}
648	  \uses{def:eulerChar_curve, def:l_invariant,
649	        lem:euler_char_shortExact_add, lem:euler_char_iso,
650	        lem:euler_char_skyscraperSheaf, def:genus,
651	        def:divisor_degree, def:divisor_closed_point, def:codim1_cycles}
652	
653	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Theorem~1.3 (the reduction inside the
654	  % proof: ``Thus we have to show that for any \(D\), $\chi(\mathscr L(D))
655	  % = \deg D + 1 - g$'') (read from
656	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
657	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Theorem 1.3 (Riemann--Roch). \emph{Let \(D\) be a
658	  % divisor on a curve \(X\) of genus \(g\). Then}
659	  % \(l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g\)."
660	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any \(D\),
661	  % \(\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g\),
662	  % where for any coherent sheaf \(\mathscr F\) on \(X\), \(\chi(\mathscr F)\)
663	  % is the Euler characteristic
664	  % \(\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)\)."
665	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Theorem~1.3, p.~295.}
666	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
667	  \(\bar k\) of genus \(g = g(C)\), and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\). Then
668	  \[
669	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g.
670	  \]
671	\end{theorem}
672	
673	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "First we consider the case \(D = 0\). Then our
674	% formula says
675	% \(\dim H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = 1 - g\),
676	% because \(H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) = k\) for any projective variety
677	% (I, 3.4), and \(\dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = g\) by (1.1).
678	% Next, let \(D\) be any divisor, and let \(P\) be any point. We will show
679	% that the formula is true for \(D\) if and only if it is true for
680	% \(D + P\). Since any divisor can be reached from \(0\) in a finite number
681	% of steps by adding or subtracting a point each time, this will show
682	% the result holds for all \(D\).
683	% We consider \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(X\). Its structure sheaf is
684	% the skyscraper sheaf \(k\) sitting at the point \(P\), which we denote by
685	% \(k(P)\), and its ideal sheaf is \(\mathscr L(-P)\) by (II, 6.18).
686	% Therefore we have an exact sequence
687	% \(0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to k(P) \to 0\).
688	% Tensoring with \(\mathscr L(D + P)\) we get
689	% \(0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0\),
690	% (since \(\mathscr L(P)\) is locally free of rank 1, tensoring by it
691	% does not affect the sheaf \(k(P)\)). Now the Euler characteristic is
692	% additive on short exact sequences (III, Ex. 5.1), and $\chi(k(P))
693	% = 1$, so we have
694	% \(\chi(\mathscr L(D + P)) = \chi(\mathscr L(D)) + 1\).
695	% On the other hand, \(\deg D + P = \deg D + 1\), so our formula is true
696	% for \(D\) if and only if it is true for \(D + P\), as required."
697	\begin{proof}
698	  \leanok
699	  \uses{def:eulerChar_curve, def:l_invariant,
700	    lem:euler_char_shortExact_add, lem:euler_char_iso,
701	    lem:euler_char_skyscraperSheaf}
702	
703	  The proof is by induction on the structure of \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) as the free
704	  abelian group on closed points (REF,
705	  REF). Both sides of the asserted
706	  equation are additive in \(D\) (the right-hand side by linearity of
707	  \(\deg\), REF; the left-hand side by the
708	  inductive step proved below), so it suffices to verify the formula
709	  for \(D = 0\) and to show that it transports across the elementary
710	  modification \(D \mapsto D + [P]\) at any closed point \(P \in C\).
711	
712	  \emph{Base case \(D = 0\).} The line bundle \(\mathcal O_C(0)\) is the
713	  structure sheaf \(\mathcal O_C\), and the formula
714	  \(\chi(\mathcal O_C) = 1 - g\) reads
715	  \[
716	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
717	    - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)
718	    \;=\; 1 - g.
719	  \]
720	  The second term is the definition of the genus,
721	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = g\) (REF). The
722	  first term is \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = 1\): \(C\) is a
723	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over the algebraically
724	  closed field \(\bar k\), so its connected components agree with its
725	  geometrically connected components, and $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
726	  \cong \bar k$ as $\bar k$-vector spaces (the Hartshorne~I.3.4
727	  statement that ``\(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X) = k\) for any projective
728	  variety'' over an algebraically closed field; the Mathlib home is
729	  the project's \(H^0\)-bridge in
730	  \texttt{Cohomology\_StructureSheafModuleK.tex}). Substituting these
731	  two values gives \(1 - g = 1 - g\), as required.
732	
733	  \emph{Inductive step (Hartshorne's ``\(D \leftrightsquigarrow D + P\)''
734	  step).} Let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. We follow Hartshorne's
735	  argument: view \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(C\); its structure
736	  sheaf is the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P) \cong \bar k\) supported at \(P\),
737	  and its sheaf of ideals is \(\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \subset \mathcal O_C\)
738	  (the locally principal ideal sheaf cutting out \(P\) in \(C\)). Hence
739	  the short exact sequence
740	  \[
741	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(-[P]) \to \mathcal O_C \to k(P) \to 0
742	  \]
743	  in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\) (Hartshorne~II.6.18). Tensoring with the
744	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) (a locally free
745	  \(\mathcal O_C\)-module of rank \(1\), so tensoring preserves exactness
746	  and leaves the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P)\) unchanged) yields
747	  \[
748	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(D)
749	    \to \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
750	    \to k(P) \to 0.
751	  \]
752	  (Strictly: tensoring \(\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \subset \mathcal O_C\) with
753	  \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) produces a sheaf isomorphic — but not
754	  syntactically equal — to \(\mathcal O_C(D)\), and the skyscraper
755	  factor \(k(P)\) is preserved up to isomorphism because
756	  \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) is locally free of rank \(1\). We transport
757	  along this isomorphism via iso-invariance of \(\chi\),
758	  REF.)
759	  Additivity of \(\chi\) on the short exact sequence
760	  (REF, the project-side
761	  factoring of the ``Now the Euler characteristic is additive on short
762	  exact sequences (III, Ex.~5.1)'' input of Hartshorne~IV.1.3) gives
763	  \[
764	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P]))
765	    \;=\;
766	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + \chi(k(P)).
767	  \]
768	  The skyscraper-sheaf input
769	  \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\) is the standalone substrate lemma
770	  REF (factored from the
771	  same Hartshorne~IV.1.3 line); its proof splits into the
772	  H\(^0\) identification \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, k(P)) = 1\) and the
773	  H\(^1\) vanishing \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\), pursued
774	  independently by the prover (see the lemma's two-paragraph proof
775	  body). Therefore
776	  \[
777	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])) \;=\; \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + 1.
778	  \]
779	  On the other hand, \(\deg([P]) = 1\) (every closed point contributes
780	  \(1\) to the bare-sum degree of REF), so by
781	  REF
782	  \[
783	    \deg(D + [P]) + 1 - g \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g + 1.
784	  \]
785	  Comparing the two displayed identities, the formula
786	  \(\chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) holds for \(D\) if and only
787	  if it holds for \(D + [P]\). Since the closed-point divisors \([P]\)
788	  generate \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) as a free abelian group, the formula propagates
789	  from \(D = 0\) to every \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) by induction on the (signed)
790	  number of generators in the expression of \(D\).
791	
792	  \emph{Lean reference note on the three substrate lemmas.} The
793	  inductive step of this proof consumes three substantive substrate
794	  lemmas, each pinned to its own Lean declaration in
795	  \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean}: the additivity
796	  REF (\(\chi\) on a short
797	  exact sequence, gated on the project-side
798	  \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured Ext-LES carrier + Grothendieck
799	  vanishing + six-term rank-counting), the iso-invariance
800	  REF (\(\chi\) on
801	  isomorphism classes, axiom-clean against Mathlib's
802	  \(\Ext\)-postcomposition API; closed iter-186), and the skyscraper
803	  identity REF
804	  (\(\chi(k(P)) = 1\), splitting into an H\(^0\) half closed against
805	  the project's existing \(H^0\)-bridge and an H\(^1\) half gated on
806	  Mathlib flasque-cohomology — see the lemma's annotated proof body).
807	  Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} provides the Euler-characteristic API
808	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.EulerCharacteristic}
809	  (\texttt{HomologicalComplex.eulerChar},
810	  \texttt{GradedObject.eulerChar}) but does \emph{not} ship the
811	  specific additivity statement
812	  \texttt{CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar\_additive} at the
813	  \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured sheaf-cohomology level the project
814	  consumes; the additivity lemma is the project-side bridge.
815	\end{proof}
816	
817	\section{The Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero}
818	
819	\begin{theorem}
820	
821	
822	\leanok
823	[Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero]
824	  \label{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}
825	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero}
826	  \uses{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus, def:eulerChar_curve,
827	        def:l_invariant}
828	
829	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Example~1.3.5 (genus-\(0\) specialisation
830	  % of Riemann--Roch; the curve-is-rational consequence) (read from
831	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314).
832	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is \emph{rational}
833	  % if it is birational to \(\mathbf P^1\) (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in
834	  % this chapter are complete and nonsingular by definition, a curve
835	  % \(X\) is rational if and only if \(X \cong \mathbf P^1\) (I, 6.12). Now
836	  % using (1.3) we can show that \(X\) is rational if and only if \(g = 0\).
837	  % We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so
838	  % conversely suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two
839	  % distinct points of \(X\) and apply Riemann--Roch to the divisor
840	  % \(D = P - Q\). Since \(\deg K - D = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have
841	  % \(l(K - D) = 0\), and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of
842	  % degree 0, so by (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words $P \sim
843	  % Q\(. But this implies \)X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
844	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
845	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
846	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) with
847	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\). Then
848	  \[
849	    \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
850	  \]
851	\end{theorem}
852	
853	\begin{proof}
854	  \leanok
855	  
856	  Specialising
857	  REF to the genus-\(0\)
858	  hypothesis \(g = 0\) yields
859	  \[
860	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
861	  \]
862	  Unfolding \(\chi\) via REF and the definition
863	  \(\ell(D) = \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\)
864	  (REF), this rewrites as
865	  \[
866	    \ell(D) - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D))
867	    \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
868	  \]
869	  Under the standing genus-\(0\) hypothesis and the assumption
870	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\), the higher-cohomology term vanishes:
871	  \[
872	    H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; 0
873	    \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0.
874	  \]
875	  Substituting yields \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\), as required.
876	
877	  \emph{The \(H^1\)-vanishing input.} On a smooth proper geometrically
878	  irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), the vanishing
879	  \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\) is the Hartshorne
880	  formulation Lemma~1.2 read covariantly: \(\ell(D) \neq 0\) forces
881	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\); equivalently, for a curve where every
882	  degree-\(0\)-or-higher invertible sheaf is generated by global
883	  sections after a single point-twist, \(H^1\) of the twist must vanish
884	  by the long exact sequence of the skyscraper extension at any
885	  closed point. In the chain of sub-build chapters, this \(H^1\)
886	  vanishing is the natural content of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
887	  \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added), where the line
888	  bundle \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) and its cohomology are constructed; once
889	  \texttt{RR.3} is closed, the present theorem inherits the
890	  vanishing as an instance, and the proof closes verbatim as
891	  displayed. Pending \texttt{RR.3}, the Lean signature
892	  \texttt{l\_eq\_degree\_plus\_one\_of\_genus\_zero} threads the
893	  \(H^1\)-vanishing hypothesis explicitly as a named premise; the
894	  iteration that closes \texttt{RR.3} then drops the premise in a
895	  follow-up signature simplification.
896	
897	  \emph{Sub-build note for \texttt{RR.4}.} The downstream consumer of
898	  this theorem is the genus-\(0\) classification
899	  REF of
900	  \texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}: with \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\)
901	  and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity
902	  (REF), Hartshorne's Example~1.3.5 chain
903	  produces a degree-\(1\) morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) via
904	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} applied to the two-dimensional
905	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\), which is then upgraded to an
906	  isomorphism in \texttt{RR.4}.
907	\end{proof}

iter-287: the blueprint-doctor `malformed_refs` of kind `literal-ref` are GENUINE render
defects — unfinished `\cref{}` cross-references left as bare `REF`/`(REF)`/`Theorem~REF`/
`REF of REF` tokens that render as literal "REF" in `leanblueprint web` HTML. NOT cosmetic.
DAG integrity rule 8 makes `literal-ref` the DAG agent's repair obligation.

**Scope governance (same as [[ts286-blueprint-doctor-mathdelim-underreports]]):** the Route-C
USER pause is prover-only (no prover lane on genus-0 `.lean`); it does NOT forbid prose fixes
on non-protected `.tex` chapters. RiemannRoch chapters are NOT in `archon-protected.yaml`.
Every `REF` lives in prose/motivation/NOTE-comment text, never in `\uses{}`/statements ⇒
**0 DAG impact** (leandag byte-identical: 878 nodes, 1484 edges, gaps 0, 2 ∞ lean-aux).

**iter-287 fixed all 80 non-protected** RiemannRoch literal-refs: `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` (43)
+ `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex` (37), via 2 scope-bounded blueprint-writers seeded with a resolved
REF→label map. 11 protected ones (`Jacobian.tex` 9, `AbelJacobi.tex` 2) stay routed to
`TO_USER.md` (auto-repair forbidden for protected files).

**Detector under-reports (confirms the [[ts286-...]] pattern for the second doctor class):**
doctor listed 35 `literal-ref` for RRFormula; actual = 37 (the two `(REF, REF)` pairs
under-counted). Reliable check = direct `grep -nE 'REF'` for standalone tokens (must be exit 1).

**Recipe for resolving a `literal-ref`:** read the block's local `\uses{}` + surrounding prose
to identify the intended target; cross-chapter "RR.1/2/3/4" = `chap:RiemannRoch_{WeilDivisor,
RRFormula,OCofP,RationalCurveIso}`; "consumed by REF of AbelianVarietyRigidity" =
`prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`. Where NO project label exists (e.g. Hartshorne 6.13(a)),
point to the nearest section (`sec:sheaf-property-correctness`) or drop the dangling token,
keeping the textbook citation. After iter-287 the blueprint carries **zero non-protected
`malformed_refs`**; `leanblueprint web` EXIT 0.

Done. Summary of iteration 287:

## What I did

Following the iter-283/iter-286 pattern of empirically testing a persistently-deferred blueprint-doctor finding rather than rubber-stamping it, I resolved the **last live doctor class** — the `literal-ref` placeholders.

- **Diagnosed** the 89 `malformed_refs` (all `literal-ref`): 78 in the two non-protected RiemannRoch chapters, 11 in protected Jacobian/AbelJacobi. These are genuine render defects — unfinished `\cref{}` cross-references left as bare `REF`/`(REF)`/`REF of REF` tokens that render as literal "REF". They were wrongly classified "cosmetic/out-of-scope" by iters 284–285 (the same mistake iter-286 corrected for `math-delim`). All live in prose, never in `\uses{}`/statements → **zero DAG impact**.
- **Resolved the REF→label mapping** myself (read both chapters, built the cross-chapter label inventory, pinned the one ambiguous cross-chapter target to `prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`), then dispatched **two scope-bounded blueprint-writers** that fixed all **80** non-protected literal-refs (OcOfD 43, RRFormula 37).
- **Independently verified**: zero `REF` tokens remain (grep exit 1); all 97 `\cref` targets resolve; marker counts unchanged (no `\label`/`\lean`/`\uses`/`\leanok`/`% SOURCE` touched); `leandag` structurally identical (878 nodes, 1484 edges, gaps 0); **`leanblueprint web` EXIT 0** with 0 "could not be resolved" crashes, both chapters re-rendered.

## Findings
- The `literal-ref` detector **also under-reports** (35 flagged vs 37 actual in RRFormula) — same pattern iter-286 found for `math-delim`. Recorded in memory `ts287-…`.
- The blueprint now carries **zero non-protected `malformed_refs`**. The only residual 11 are in mathematician-protected files (auto-repair forbidden) — routed to `TO_USER.md`.

## Status
`in_progress` — gate **6/7 PASS**; criterion 5 (54 lean-aux 1-to-1) stays **structurally deferred** (2 are ∞ prover-stuck pieces; covering them would trade a criterion-5 fail for a criterion-1 fail). STRATEGY.md unchanged; prover lane byte-frozen since iter-277. The blueprint is not the bottleneck — the stuck-ness is a Lean-kernel `whnf`/`eqToHom` wall on D3′/dual-route-2, for the plan/prover phase.

Wrote `DAG_STATUS.md`, `iter/iter-287/dag.md`, and the memory entry. Skips (strategy-critic, blueprint-reviewer, dag-walker, progress-critic, effort-breaker) recorded with rationale.
